deepDive.title: Does VLA Even Know the Basics? Measuring Commonsense and World Knowledge Retention in Vision-Language-Action Models
deepDive.summary: This paper is a multi agent systems lead from the research stream. Embodied Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models are typically obtained by fine-tuning powerful pretrained VLMs on robotics data, yet it is unclear how much commonsense and factual knowledge they retain after adaptation. It gets the longer read because the public context is source-grounded and the item is close to the systems layer.
bullets: What happened: This paper is a multi agent systems lead from the research stream. Embodied Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models are typically obtained by fine-tuning powerful pretrained VLMs on robotics data, yet it is unclear how much commonsense and factual knowledge they retain after adaptation.

Why it matters: The systems angle is multi agent systems; the useful question is whether the method changes reliability, runtime behavior, governance, or infrastructure constraints. The public metadata is strong enough for a compact self-contained read.
